title: HONKID Esc Keycap for Mechanical Gaming Keyboard Switches, Custom Keycaps for Esc and Tab Key with Magnetic Light Macintosh Retro Style Design (White)
features: TRIBUTE TO THE CLASSICS: The retro computer keycaps is in honor of the the classic 1984 Macintosh computers, the unique retro styling of ESC keycap and Tab keycap give you a new experience

COMPATIBILITY: Uinversal mechanical keyboard Cross interface, compatible with most MX switches as cherry MX, kailh, outemu, gateron, ect. Our custom keycaps fit most keyboards especially 60% mechanical gaming keyboard, the keycaps include ESC keycap and Tab keycap

LIGHT-TRANSMISSION DESIGN: Unique light guide design, the light transmission is brighter and more uniform. If your keyboard has a backlight function, then your light will be delivered through the keycap card, and the effect is perfect. Five classic card patterns, which can be changed at will

MAGNETIC DESIGN: Both ESC and Tab keycap have magnetic, simple and stable.You can attach the mini keyboard to any keycap, card panel of the keycap also uses the magnetic function, which makes it very convenient and fast to replace the translucent cards
DELICATE EXQUISITE: You will feels better and more delicate and comfortable,The delicate matte texture and the details of the back are enough to make you miss the first generation of MAC

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

title: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Wireless/USB Wired Walnut Wood Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard for PC/Mac, Walnut Wood (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US) & RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)
description: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Luxury Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard, Metallic Brown/Grey (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US)Inspired by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present and future. The retro classic is a backlit mechanical keyboard with round keycaps that evokes nostalgia of yesteryear complemented with a durable aluminum frame and a sumptuous genuine leather or walnut surface.Azio RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)The retro classic mouse (RCM) stays true to the minimalist philosophy in order to accentuate its ambidextrous design. With its modern features and ability to work on virtually any surface, The RCM is an essential companion for your retro classic & compact keyboard.
features: Product 1: A classic reborn: design originated by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present, and future.
Product 1: Genuine & luxurious: Genuine leather or wood top plate. Leather reflects taste, craftsmanship, and exclusiveness while emitting a unique charm that inspires.
Product 1: Tactile. Clicky. Backlit: the backlit mechanical keys are tuned to be tactile and "clicky", reminiscent of vintage typewriters.
Product 1: Forged. Polished. Plated: The keyboard frame is forged with aluminum alloy and plated into a beautiful chrome/Satin finish to accompany the leather top plate.
Product 2: GENUINE LEATHER SURFACE - Utilizing top-grain chrome-tanned leather to provide a pleasant touch, each leather piece is precision-cut, molded in, and hand-trimmed to perfection.

Product 2: EXQUISITE CRAFTMANSHIP - Immaculate attention to every detail - Genuine leather top cover, textured aluminum wheel and alloy frame with precision bolts. The scroll wheel design starts with a high-grade aluminum rod that is knurled into a diamond-shaped pattern and completed with chamfered edges on a precision CNC lathe. It is then anodized to enhance aesthetics and durability.
Product 2: TRACKS Virtually ANYWHERE - The PixArt PAW3805 sensor is designed to track flawlessly on virtually any surface, including glass and other high-gloss materials.

Product 2: DUAL USB-RF & BLUETOOTH - Interfaces Connect wirelessly with Bluetooth or RF via USB receiver. Works on Bluetooth enabled PC's natively but also includes a USB RF receiver for computers without Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Check Your Computer First
reviews.text:
I sure hated to send this beauty back to Amazon, but I gave it a fair trial and I'm just not satisfied, and here's why.
It's a beautiful piece of machinery/artwork and weighs in at about 10 lbs. The keyboard glows in such a way that it will illuminate your work area in a dark room, and you can adjust the brightness up or down or off.
No, my complaint is with the speed at which it reacts. This keyboard has to keep up with you, possibly in order to mimic an old fashioned Royal or Underwood. I'm used to a $30 Logitech wireless that I can wag to the kitchen with my laptop. I don't like to use the laptop keyboard and scroll pad, I like a separate keyboard, it's faster for me. I find myself correcting errors constantly, and it slows me down, but I'm no stenographer either.
This Azio is Bluetooth, but not all computers support it. My 6 year old Toshiba doesn't, so I have to use the USB connection cord and block a precious port full time.
What a shame, it sure is elegant, and a lot of folks won't care about the perceived lag time.
The star rating posed a problem for me because I'm trying to be honest about my needs and I realize another might be perfectly satisfied.
This brute is expensive, and I'm not inclined to spend north of $700 for a new computer when I don't need one, just to support it.

reviews.title: Good keyboard with one minor catch
reviews.text: Very good keyboard, but it is heavier than I thought.  The height is not easier adjusted so I have to leave my drop down keyboard tray stuck out.

reviews.title: Great Keyboard - Bad Reliability Plus Non-Existent Support
reviews.text: Ordered this roughly a year ago. Started having problems with the mouse within a few months, with the mouse not holding a charge. Got the product replaced under warranty. Replacement mouse never would connect via the USB dongle consistently. 10 months in or so, my keyboard started acting up and, despite being plugged in via USB-C on my desk, continually shows out of power / red light. When I move it to Bluetooth it strangely works, so it can't be out of power. Reached out to Azio support and got crickets. Now have a nearly $200 set of pretty chrome and leather and plastic paperweight. Looks great, fabulous when it work. Sadly that's not very consistently or long. I've got cheap keyboards that lasted multiple years easily.

reviews.title: Not Bad
reviews.text: The keyboard and mouse was packed well for the shipping and arrived in great shape.  Very tactile feel and old school look.

reviews.title: Incorrect Description Information - Wrong item
reviews.text: First off it is very cute and I loved it, but the description says wireless/wired, giving the indication that it can be used wireless and it cannot.  This is a lot of money for a keyboard that has to be plugged in.  Also, I can’t tell how to get in touch with the seller so I can get confirmation.  RETURNING.

title: EPOMAKER Macaron 1.8m Coiled Type-C to USB A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable with Detachable Aviator Connector for Gaming Keyboard/Tablet/Smart Phone (Purple)
features: 【Epomaker Macaron Cable For Keyboards and More】 To provide more options of cables, Epomaker custom Macaron cable is designed to match your keyboard, no matter what type of setup you’re working with. Chose from our different color options, it comes easy for you to match your keyboard and top off your setup. Besides that, this cable features all you want and need: Double-shielded USB 2.0 compliant, 61-gauge, 4-conductor, etc.

【Durable and Flexible】Compared with EPOMAKER MX cable, the Macaron cable adopts more softer material thus you may get a confortable experience. They are both durable and flexible due to the strict selection of the durable PP double-sleeves raw materials. This structure enables the cable to be protected from fracture. The coiled design allows the cables easily to integrate into a wide range of spaces

【USB-C Interface, USB 2.0/3.0 Compatible】For versatile connectivity, each one is equipped with a detachable aviator port to accommodate different connectors. Included in this run is a USB-C connector cable to fit modern boards

【Six Colors Choices】The cable is designed in various light colors, inspiring from the dessert Macaron, to match different keyboard trend: yellow, light green, blue, pink and etc. Epomaker custom Macaron cable adds more vibrant energy to your daily life and work. Get it to refresh your keyboard right away!

reviews.title: It looks so much cooler!
reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

reviews.title: Clean key caps
reviews.text: Using these key caps on a Corsair K70 pro mini wireless and they look great. They feel good and match my purple and white theme

title: Spacebar Keycap,DIY PTB Keycaps,Five-Sided Sublimation Custom Keycaps,6.25U 6.25X Space Keycap OEM Profile Keycaps for DIY Mechanical Keyboard - A Variety of Personalized Patterns Optional (kit11)
features: 【DIY Space Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboard】This keycap kit include Space Keycap and a Keycap Puller, PBT Keycaps, a good choice for you to DIY your mechanical keyboard.

【PBT Keycaps】This PBT keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. The zero-shine matte look will last for as long as you use it. This keycap has high color fastness, wear resistance, and no fading.

Specially designed for mechanical keyboard, exquisite workmanship, easy to install and use.If you already hate the same solid color, it must be a good choice to embellish your keyboard with some patterns.

reviews.title: Quality Keycaps
reviews.text: Quality keycaps.  Well made, and form fitting. Great colors, and good quality.

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming

title: G.SKILL USB KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard, Cherry MX Red, ABS Dual Injection Keycap, (Black)
description: The G.SKILL KM360 tenkeyless mechanical keyboard is a compact 87-key layout keyboard designed for durability. Featuring long-lasting Cherry MX mechanical keyswitches, wear-proof ABS double injection keycaps, and a solid aluminum top-plate, the KM360 mechanical keyboard is the perfect daily mechanical keyboard. With a detachable USB Type-C cable, it's easy to replace a worn cable by simply swapping it out with another USB Type-C cable of your choice.
features: CHERRY MX - Most popular mechanical keyswitch for their high standards of quality, reliability, and durability; rated for over 50 million keypresses.
COMPACT & PORTABLE - Designed with a detachable USB Type-C cable and a tenkeyless layout for more desk space and great portability.
WEAR-PROOF KEYS - Using ABS dual-injection keycaps mean the keys won't wear out like printed or laser etched keycaps.
HIGH DURABILITY - Constructed with a solid aluminum top plate and floating keyswitches for easy cleaning.
MODERN DESIGN - Featuring ergonomic keys, white LED backlight, and n-key rollover for comfort and ease of use.

reviews.title: Clicky clack who’s at the rack with the jackets? Me!
reviews.text:
I had been eyeing a mechanical keyboard for years, hesitant to buy one because A) I already had a perfectly fine working keyboard and B) Reviews I had read made it sound like they were so noisy you needed to wear ear protection while using one. During a recent move I left my previously mentioned perfectly fine working keyboard outside for an extended period of time, uncovered in the rain and sun and any other elements that rolled though Southern California for those few months. When I finally returned to the keyboard, I found that it had become yet another casualty of the pandemic. Finally I had good cause for a new keyboard, and my research led me to try this one. And like the famous tattoo says, I have no ragrets!

I plugged it in and immediately it lit up and off my little fingies went, zipping around the keyboard with the grace and elegance of a gold medal winning gymnast at the 2021 Tokyo Olympics. Every click and clack was like music to my ears, each sentence I typed sounding like a symphony composed by Mozart himself! I now look forward to typing my weekly emails to mother, catching her up with her favorite son’s (sorry Richard, but you know it’s true!) comings and goings!
I’ve only had this keyboard for about a week, so time will tell if it will hold up to the rigorous poundings that accompany each email I compose, but as of now I would not hesitate to recommend this keyboard to all my many (ok, zero) friends! Now I can’t wait to get off from my day job as a clerk typist and go home and get some typing in!

reviews.title: Great Initial Impression
reviews.text:
My first foray into mechanical KB and cherry switches. I was familiar with GSkill as they are a trusted brand in performance PC DDR4 RAM. I liked the price point and the size was perfect. Smaller than a full size but still having arrow keys and delete, etc off to the side.
I’m no KB expert, but it appears to be of good quality and finish. Nice adjustable LED backlighting. It has a sleeved nylon cable that’s white and matches the board nicely. Typing on it will take some getting used to, but I’m liking it so far and will update later if there’s any issues.

reviews.title: Amazing keyboard EDIT:
reviews.text: Amazing keyboard.  I'm typing on my new G.SKILL KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard and YES it amazing!  Seriously if you have the money by this product.  Totally worth every penny! It's been just over a year since I got this mechanical keyboard & the USB type C is coming loose, I have to move the cord back N forth so it will connect again. Not sure if it can be fixed or not. (EDIT) I got this in October of 2019 & the small side of the USB Type-C female side is breaking off, causing the keyboard to flicker on and off. Other than that it's awesome keyboard

reviews.title: Great keyboard from a regular joe
reviews.text:
This is my first keyboard and saw great review. I’m not a big gamer or anything but recently started playing a little on a gaming laptop and thought why not. I thought the keyboard on the laptop were fine but damn these are better. I started using them for work and everything and love this key board now. So if your on the fence just do it.

10/10

reviews.title: Almost perfect
reviews.text: Exactly what I was looking for in an TKL board. The only thing that’s below standard are the kick stands. They don’t provide much resistance, so when you just nudge the keyboard forward the kickstands collapse - not sure if they need to be more sturdy or have a more grippy rubber foot but a rather disappointing feature to an otherwise perfect set up.

title: dagaladoo XVX 189 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, PBT Custom Keyboard Keycaps Full Set, XVX Profile Keycaps for 60% 65% 70% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Black/Purple
features: Full 189 keys PBT keycaps set, including 108 standard keys, 61 supplementary keys and 20 invert color supplementary keys for most 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
XVX profile keycaps, ergonomic height design similar than Cherry profile, while increasing the contact surface between the finger and keycaps, bring you a comfortable typing experience.
Made from high content PBT material, 0.2cm extra-thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching, with a large number of personalized complement keys, you can DIY different styles of keyboards according to your own ideas.

reviews.title: Fits Royal Kludge 90% and Keychron K10!
reviews.text: I like all the extras. The quality of the PBT is grainy in texture, so if that's the kind of thing you're in to, then these won't disappoint. Not shine through, although the advertisement never claims they are. No light is coming through these from your board. The XVX profile is nice. Many extra key options- this set has fit several boards from several brands- not something you see for the price point! Quality printing and nice font on the caps. The keys feel good to type on when used with a clicky or linear switch. They are a bit heavy, so a lighter switch wouldn't be a bad option. I don't like to use these on tactile switches due to the mushy that these caps add to the keys. O Rings did not help these keys IMO.

reviews.title: Random assortment of loose keys
reviews.text: Expected a full set of keycaps, instead received a box containing three bags of loose keys, missing multiple letters, numbers, function keys, etc. Might be good for a concept art project, but not for a working keyboard.

reviews.title: Some keys misaligned with switch stems.
reviews.text: The keys have a nice touch, sound and printing however some keys, particularly my "O" key are a misaligned with the switch stem causing it to stick up a little higher than the "I" and "P" on either side. I did not mistake a "0" for an "O".

reviews.title: Seem to be good quality, but I hate the XVX profile
reviews.text:
These seems to be good quality keycaps, I have no issues with them at all. I have put them on multiple boards now, and I haven't had any break or scratch. Colors are accurate and legends are clear. No complaints there.

My one gripe is the XVX profile. It takes a lot of getting used to (like any new profile would), but the surface area of the keys is much larger than most other profiles. Therefore, when typing, it feels as if your fingers have to travel significantly further to reach the next key. It also makes it much, much easier to fat finger extra keys. If I had realized how much more surface area there was, I would not have purchased these. However, they do look great, they were cheap, and they sound pretty good too.

reviews.title: Great keycaps, andd even better customer service!
reviews.text: I wanted to find some PBT keycaps that were double-shot and gave some aesthetic flair to my $15 108 full sized mech keyboard with brown switches.  They fit just fine, and give a different, yet, pleasing sounds and feel to the keyboard.  I wanted non-see through keycaps in order to hide most of the LED rainbow glare.  The backlit effect is, again, very pleasing.  The keycap set that I originally ordered was missing the F9 key.  I contacted their customer service through Amazon, and got a reply back right away and arranged for a replacement set to be shipped out.  Got it the new set the day after, and it was complete.  Will purchase from this seller again whenever I need/want new keycaps.  Very confident if something is wrong, will be taken care of in a timely matter.

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: NPKC Blank Thick PBT OEM Profile 108 ANSI Keycaps for MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard (Only Keycap) (Dark Blue)
description:
This link is only the keycaps,no keyboard included.The keycap loading board is only to take photo,not included in the sold package.We will pack the keycaps in our own carton.

reviews.title: High Quality Caps - Adore the Theme
reviews.text: As a typist, these caps have a great feel to them. They are thick without being heavy. No wobble. Improve the sound of the board and switch. I purchased the Smoke Cloud theme, and the images are adorable for a weather enthusiast like me. This set also had plenty of extra keys to complete the 96% keyboards, which not a lot of sets include. I was leary of paying $40 for a set without trying them first, but I don't regret it - great purchase - fast shipping!

reviews.title: Looks great, sounds like quality
reviews.text: I'm a bit of a keycap junkie.  I love being able to swap keys out to give my keyboard a different look.  If you're looking at this, you likely already like the look and I think they look better in person.  The keycaps are easy to install and provide good sound.  No hollow cheapness to these!

reviews.title: Very thick keycaps
reviews.text:
What I like
I really like the quality of the prints on these keycaps.
Also the thickness of these keycaps are even past some doubleshot ones I have.

What I find questionable.
The character keys looked like they were a light green color, but they are bright white. I ended up liking them anyway, just was a bit surprised.

Overall they are a great set of keycaps, going to keep using them for sure.

Great little set.

This keyboard looks and works good. The back lighting makes it easy to see in the dark and the keys are precise and feel good while typing. The real aluminum frame is sturdy and looks good too. The phone holder is a nice touch. I would say it's worth it for the price.

reviews.title: Not for Certain Noise
reviews.text:
Unfortunately these o-rings didn't help with the noise from my mechanical keyboard and switches. The noise from my keyboard comes from the sound the springs make when the button pops back up, and these o-rings work to muffle sound when the keys are pressed down. For keyboards with raised keys that don't bottom out, like mine, and for switches that make more noise when they come back up instead of when they go down, the o-rings are not effective for reducing noise.

Otherwise, the product is good, and I'm keeping the o-rings for when I eventually change out the switches on my keyboard entirely.

reviews.title: awesome!
reviews.text: awesome set of o-rings for the price.

reviews.title: Really nice modification for my keyboard
reviews.text: These cut down the travel and damp the clicks in my moonlander, great value.

reviews.title: Good value rings kit
reviews.text:
I was setting up two keyboards with lighter switches, and think that o-rings are important when you drop the force. This kit was a good value, and I like the brushes to clean the keyboards.

I would recommend a decent set of tweezers to push the rings down onto the key posts. I had a plastic set that was perfect for it ( they came with a pair of Moondrop IEMs ).

reviews.title: Almost perfect, if not for the competition
reviews.text:
TLDR: If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering the Wooting60HE or just really like Steelseries products, this is a really good 60% option.

Pros:
-Omnipoint switches feel very good
-Adjustable actuation point
-Finally adopted PBT keycaps for longevity
-Good RGB brightness and accuracy
-Pretty good stabs out of the box made better with some lube

Cons:
-The Wooting60HE exists...

Thoughts:
Rant time. Right out of the gate, this would be the perfect 60% keyboard currently, if the Wooting60HE didn't exist. It has hotswap switches, acoustic foam, analog functionality, adjustable actuation point, RAPID TRIGGER (huge for high actuation point users), pre-lubed switches and stabs, and doesn't require its software to be active to function fully. This board isn't bad by any means, it's just that the wooting is just better in my opinion. I saw steelseries' video today comparing their "response time" with some weird rigged comparison test where they claim a 10ms advantage over the wooting. Even if that were true, which I don't think is accurate, 10ms isn't enough to justify choosing this over the wooting, unless you just really like Steelseries products, which is fine. But the rapid trigger functionality alone is HUGE for switches with a high actuation. If you don't know what that is, basically at any point during the upward travel (after you've pressed a key), you can press down again, to register another keypress. It basically makes the actuation dynamic letting you keep that high actuation, but not require you to fully release the key to press it again. Anyway, rant/PSA over, back to the Apex.

Despite everything I just said, this is still a really good keyboard. If you liked the Apex Pro/TKL, you'll like this. I've found that magnetic switches are easily the smoothest ones on the market, even without lubing. They just glide, absolutely fantastic.

I would comment on the responsiveness, but honestly, I really can't imagine milliseconds making a huge difference for movement inputs in a shooter. Osu probably if that's your thing, but starting to move in one direction or another a milisecond faster isn't going to change your game in the slightest. A mouse, absolutely, but there's really no keyboard input that needs that level of precision. So try not to get hung up on responsiveness in regards to keyboard claims.

Something definitely worth noting is how magnetic switches and optical switches are basically immune to double presses that eventually happen to regular mechanical switches. You can rest assure that all of your key presses will be very accurate.

Conclusion:
Before you commit to this, I highly recommend looking into the Wooting60HE. If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering, or just really like Steelseries products, this is still a really good keyboard. I love the switches, and am so happy they decided to finally start using PBT keycaps.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: I don’t know why people are hating on this keyboard so much? unless you’re like a nerd and actually care about the .2mm and stuff about the price. The keys so really nice and smooth and is a very quiet keyboard, if you want to drop this much for a keyboard then go for it i think it was a good purchase for me

reviews.title: Perfect for Gaming
reviews.text: 15 year old gamer approved! It allows for quicker response times in games, lights are bright and stay on. The keys are a little noisy but if you’re into ASMR, it’s relaxing!

reviews.title: Takes some getting used to.
reviews.text: This mini keyboard was a major change from my previous keyboard. The first major shock I had when I needed the grave/tilde key for programming, thankfully with the SteelSeries GG program I was able to program the grave/tilde key into the escape key with a modifier. In Minecraft as well some of the F3 functions needed to be mapped because there is no function row. After some getting used to I am just as fast on this keyboard as my previous one. There was a quirk I could not figure out without some googling however, if you hold the SteelSeries logo and the windows button it locks the windows button and changes it's color to white. Doing it again will re-enable the windows button.

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

reviews.title: Great Keycaps for the price!!

Usually on cheaper sets like this the legending and print quality can vary quite a bit but these keycaps were really consistent and is on par with some other sets I've had that cost twice as much so that was nice to see. Overall they're pretty great for the price and I honestly recommend them if you if you're looking for "designer" style keycaps.

reviews.title: Beautiful keycaps for designers!
reviews.text: These keycaps are beautiful! I use photoshop often so I already have the shortcuts memorized but having these keycaps are just so nice regardless. They feel really nice and thick, the printing on them is nice and clear, and I love the color way for them. I dont use this board often even when working in photoshop and really just use this board and keycap set as a decorative piece to add to my collection of keyboards. I love these keycaps!!

title: VELOCIFIRE Keycap Puller Tool, 3 in 1 Keycap Puller, Stainless Steel Keycap Removal Tool, Key Pulling, Loosen, Tighten Keycap Stem, Expert for Mechanical Keyboard(Black)
features: This wire style keycap puller is a necessary tool for any mechanical keyboard owner. Easily remove and replace keycaps with this keycap removal tool.
Creative two-stage combination design, multifunctional keycap puller, one part is keycap puller, one part is keycap stem adjust.
You just need to place the keycap puller metal at the bottom of the keycap and pull the keycap out vertically.
Round body shape designed for maximum comfort.Compatible for most mechanical keyboards including Cherry MX，Kailh, Outemu, and Content Switches.
Wires can bend outward to fit onto every key except the spacebar. (The spacebar key can be removed by hand after the surrounding keys are removed).

reviews.title: It's good, but it doesn't fit Daskeyboard keys
reviews.text: I have a das keyboard.  This doesn't fit over the keys.  I had to twist and bend it to make it work.  It's not the right tool for a das keyboard.  With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.

title: Wireless Gaming Keyboard and Mouse Combo, 104 Keys Standard US Layout RGB Backlit Rechargeable Mechanical Feel Waterproof Mute Mouse for Gamers(White)
features: 【2 in 1 wireless keyboard and mouse set】：This combo includes a 104-key Standard American layout gaming keyboard and a light-up gaming mouse. The keyboard and mouse both support charging, and a common charging cable is included in the package.

【2.4Ghz wireless connection】：The keyboard and mouse share a dongle, the dongle is located at the bottom of the mouse, the maximum support for 32 feet using distance. (Please open the keyboard and mouse switch before use)
【RGB backlit keyboard】 : Keyboard is equipped with FN+(1-5) 5 groups of shortcut keys to adjust the keyboard lighting in 19 color modes. Keyboard brightness adjustable, night use is not affected.

【Wireless Gaming Mouse】: This wireless mouse features a cool crackle pattern and 7-color auto-change breathing backlight design, 7 buttons, symmetrical design of the mouse, and 20 million keystrokes life.

【Plug and Play】:The keyboard and mouse are factory charged and paired, just turn on the keyboard and mouse switch and insert the dongle to use. (Note: If you lose the connection, please complete the charge, reset the pairing code pairing can be)

reviews.title: Pretty, but this WILL break within 3 months
reviews.text: Very low quality. I've replaced everything once before and now need another but no warranty.

reviews.title: Needs a dimmer or more contrast
reviews.text: Hard to see the key characters when lit

reviews.title: Nice Product
reviews.text: I recommend the black keyboard, for one reason: it lights up the keys, showing the letters, and the numbers. With the white keyboard, and the light it just doesn't show the letters and numbers as easily because the light against the white is too much alike. I returned this for this reason only: great buying/returning experience. I recommend this seller. The product is very nice, and of good quality.

reviews.title: Broken Already...
reviews.text: I bought this back in October 2022. Off and on switch broke off. Keyboard is stuck in the on position.

reviews.title: Lasted two months. Performed great for two months suddenly died.
reviews.text: Loved until it died an early death.

title: ikbc W200 Wireless Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switch for Windows and Mac OS, Enables Media Key and LED Indicator (2.4G Dongle, USB 2.0, PBT Double Shot 87 Keycaps, Black Color, ANSI/US)
features: ▼ Mechanical keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switches (Linear): A linear, lighter but does not have tactile bump and audible click found comparing with Cherry MX Blue switches. Cherry MX Red is ideal for gaming where the low articulation force and linear path allow for quick successive key presses. Typing with Cherry MX Red switches is similar to browns, though it does lack tactile feedback and It is always the best preference for gamers.
▼ A durable computer keyboard with PBT Double Shot keycaps for Ergonomic Keycap Shapes designed to provide you with durable, long lasting and comfortable typing experience.

▼ Plug-And-Play Simplicity. : Setup takes seconds—you simply plug the wireless receiver into a USB port and start using your keyboard right away without software. 100% Anti-ghosting with 6-key Rollover and convenient FN Multimedia Shortcuts Keys.

▼ Other Features : Original Cherry MX Switch-Each Reliable Switches Rated for 50 million Typing Times. Using 2.4Ghz Wireless can Connectivity-Up to 6 Month Long Standby Time. Product structure include the Smart Power Indicator Light and 2 Adjustable Feetstand Height.
▼ What You Can Get: iKBC Cherry MX Red switch mechanical keyboard x1, 2.4G wireless dongle x1 (equipped inside battery area), AAA batteries x2, dust cover x1, key puller x1, user manual x1.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text:
It meets all my needs. I got it to replace a wireless Velocifire keyboard whose internal battery was dying. I had to plug my old one in everyday as I couldn’t see the key labels without the backlight, and it’s built in battery life decreased from 5 to 1 days over a couple years. So it was essentially wired, while I prefer the wireless aesthetic.

This one avoids the backlight killing the battery life as well as internal battery degradation. I can see the key labels fine without a backlight, and it takes replaceable batteries. Battery life should be months without the backlight, and it even has a low battery warning light. No cord to deal with.

Seems well built, heavy, and doesn’t slide on the desk. Nice price point. Easy to set up. It even has multiple tilt angle (height) options, although I like it flat. TKL is great to minimize the distance to reach the mouse, improving ergonomics.

I went from brown to red switches as that’s all that was in stock, and it was an easy change, although I could tell a slight difference initially.

reviews.title: Quite Loud
reviews.text:
If you can't stand the sound of a clacking keyboard, this isn't for you. It can get quite loud, especially when you're typing fairly quickly. This is probably better for techies who like the intense sound and the tactile key interface. The lighting is also nice. I can recommend this keyboard for those who either live or work alone.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided helps make your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: A No-Cable Classic For Writers
reviews.text:
I am not lying: This is hands down the best keyboard for writing I have used in the last twenty years. I liked the older acrylic Apple keyboard and I own a mechanical keyboard with click-switches (blue), but this one makes touch typing so much more comfortable.
I wasn’t sure if I would like the tactile keys without click, because I actually like the click. They are just very loud. Logitech’s K845 (with Cherry MX red) is a great keyboard as well, but louder and has a cable.
I wish this keyboard would be backlit though. Black keyboards in front of big screens need some sort of illumination. I got one of these USB LED lights that I plugged behind my monitor, that half-solves the problem by providing some backlight.
Still it’s awesome to write on it. It’s focus is to be a mechanical keyboard. So much that it seems that it skipped the product design. It looks like an OEM-Keyboard from 1995. That’s in a way uncompromisingly functional, but next to a modern flat butterfly keyboard it looks like someone parked an old Volvo next to a Miata.
The quality is good. The keycaps are heavy and lettering sharp. It might be confusing for some that the ‘upper’ characters are printed next to the main set in the same size.
Battery life seems not to be a problem. I am pretty much writing all day, and I’m still on the first set of batteries that came in the package.
PROS AND CONS
+ Precise keyboard for touch-typing
+ Quiet enough for shared work areas
+ Height adjustable
+ Great quality
+ Long Battery life
+ Connects via dongle, not Bluetooth (yes, for me that’s a pro. Bluetooth sucks)
+ Compact with no number pad

- Chunky design
- No backlight
- Very heavy
- Vague keycap lettering
- No number pad (*conflict*)
I can recommend this keyboard to anyone who is typing over a long amount of times. It takes a while to get used to, but once adjusted it’s less tiring than modern butterfly or rubber cap keyboards, because the keys don’t bottom out that easily, and feel better to the fingertips. I took a few photos. I hope it helps to make a decision.

reviews.title: Silent Cherry Red Switches Work Well With Family Room Computer
reviews.text:
While the cherry MX red keys are often used for gaming, I am reviewing this as a typing keyboard in a family room, where the loud clackity clack of a keyboard is unwelcome. And I find this keyboard to be a great solution for such an environment.

This keyboard paired so effortlessly with my Mac that I wasn’t even aware it happened. I checked the keys for their mapping, and found them to be as one would expect for a Windows keyboard—just the usual issue with the non-Apple standard keys near the Shift key.

It is not “full-size,” but 80% size, so it is somewhat smaller, but not so much so that you fee cramped.

They keys are as silent as advertised. And while an old-school touch typist might miss the “click” feedback of a mechanical keyboard, these keys have decent travel, so if you are typing, you will have little doubt that you have actually engaged the key. (I can’t talk to gaming with these.)

I have had no trouble with the bluetooth connection, nor the responsiveness while typing.

If I have any reliability issues, I will be sure to revisit this review as necessary.
